frameworks.name: sensory preconditioning
frameworks.one_line: neutral stimulus paired with another inherits acquired reward value

frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: MVPA-driven fMRI feedback induces a non-conscious target representation

frameworks.name: model-based learning
frameworks.one_line: framework typically used to explain sensory preconditioning effects

core_question: Can sensory preconditioning form an association when one of the paired representations is never consciously perceived?
key_assumptions: MVPA likelihood of a target category reflects a covert visual representation
neurofeedback can shape BOLD patterns toward a target representation without awareness
betting choices index acquired value of a stimulus
manipulations: viewed dot-motion display paired with a non-conscious DecNef target category
later reward reinforcement attached to the dot-motion display
key_findings: participants came to value the dot-motion display, which had been linked to an unseen target category via neurofeedback
sensory preconditioning emerged despite the paired target representation never being consciously seen
interpretations: sensory preconditioning, and by extension model-based associative learning, can operate over representations outside conscious awareness
